rulings.Case Title: VLADIMIR NOSACH VS. HENRY EPSTEIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application For Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 29, 2015, Line 2, PETITIONER VLADIMIR NOSACH'S Application For Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Denied. CCP sec. 1094.5 confers jurisdiction on the superior court to inquire into the validity of any final administrative order or decision. Case law also establishes that an administrative decision must be final before the Court has jurisdiction to rule under CCP sec. 1094.5. (See Kumar v. National Medical Enterprises, Inc. (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1050, 1055, "A party must go through the entire proceeding to a final decision on the merits of the entire controversy before resorting to the courts for relief... An appeal can only be taken only from a final decision aggrieving petitioner;" Hongsathavij v. Queen of Angels/Hollywood Presbyterian Medical Center (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 1123, 1135, "the role of the superior court in reviewing [a writ petition brought pursuant to CCP Sec. 1094.5(a)] is to inquire 'into the validity of any final administrative order or decision;" Board of Med. Quality Assur. v. Sup. Ct. (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 860, 862, "section 1094.5 deals with the final review of administrative orders of decisions.") Here, there has been no final administrative decision or ruling. Therefore, the writ of administrative mandamus is denied without prejudice to Petitioner's ability to seek mandamus relief once the administrative proceeding has been exhausted. The Court may issue a writ of traditional mandamus under CCP sec. 1085 to compel the performance of an act that the law specially enjoins as a duty from an office. Generally, the petitioner must show that there is no other plain, speedy, and adequate remedy, that the respondent has failed to perform an act despite a clear, present, and ministerial duty to do so, and that the petitioner has a clear, present, and beneficial right to that performance. (E.g., Riverside Sheriff's Ass'n v. County of Riverside (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 1285, 1289.) Here, Petitioner has not filed a petition under sec. 1085. The Court may, and does, construe the petition as one for traditional mandate in the alternative. Traditional mandamus relief, however, is denied. Petitioner has a plain, speedy, and adequate remedy - a ruling from the hearing officer, exhaustion of administrative review and appropriate judicial review under sec. 1094.5. Further, Petitioner has not demonstrated a breach of a mandatory, ministerial duty. The petition is denied and the matter is remanded back to the administrative hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN W. WHITE et al VS. PRESIDENTIAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al (Defts: Montoya and JLM Challenge Judge Quinn)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 29, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANTS PRESIDENTIAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, ATHENE ANNUINTY & LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY, AND ATHENE ANNUITY & LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Part 1 of 2 For Purposes of Entry of Tentative Ruling Only). The court denies Defendants John Montoya and JLM Wealth Strategies, Inc.'s joinder request as untimely. The moving Defendants' motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication in the alternative is denied. Defendants fail to maintain its initial burden of production on the professional negligence, negligence, elder abuse, and rescission causes of action. Defendants and Mr. Montoya violated their statutory duties under Insurance Code secs. 10509.914 and 10509.915. Defendants are liable for Mr. Montoya's conduct. Annuities are regulated as a form of life insurance. See Ins. Code Sec. 101. Only licensed life agents are authorized to sell annuity products. See Ins. Code Secs. 32, 1622, 1626. A "life agent" is a person authorized by and on behalf of a life, disability, or life and disability insurer to transact life, disability, or life and disability insurance. See Ins. Code 1622. Defendants rely on inapposite auto insurance cases to argue that Mr. Montoya was decedent's agent. Defendants never trained Mr. Montoya (Montoya Depo., 42:23-43:5; 62:19-24) and Mr. Montoya did not properly explain the import of decedent's "rated age" to decedent. (Montoya depo., 52:4-24). Plaintiffs' expert (Mr. Granger) explains the misunderstanding at par. 44 of his declaration. See also (Wiener Depo., 90:3-23). Defendants cite Das v. Bank of America, N.A. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 727, for the proposition that plaintiff cannot rely on various sections of the Insurance Code to establish negligence per se because those provisions do not provide for a private right of action. In Das, the court ruled that the plaintiff could not rely on a reporting statute because the statute contained a provision affirmatively foreclosing the use of the reporting duty for purposes of a negligence claim. In this case, the Insurance Code provisions contain no such language and the general rule of negligence per se applies. (See Nativi v. Deutsche Bank National Trust Company (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 261, 286 [existence of an express or implied private right of action is not necessary to a negligence claim based on a statutory violation].) Defendants' statements were misleading and support liability on the elder abuse and rescission claims. "A person or entity shall be deemed to have taken, secreted, appropriated, obtained, or retained property for a wrongful use if, among other things, the person or entity takes, secretes, appropriates, obtains, or retains the property and the person or entity knew or should have known that this conduct is likely to be harmful to the elder ...." Id. at 736 (emphasis in original). The court denies summary adjudication on Plaintiffs' statutory bad faith claim. Plaintiffs' claim is based on Insurance Code sec. 785. Section 785 is written in a way that suggests that a private right of action may be maintained. Secondary sources suggest that a private right of action may be maintained. (See Croskey, et al., Cal. Practice Guide: Insurance Litigation (The Rutter Group (updated Nov. 2014) Paragraphs 11:370 - 11:378.) And, Defendants have directed the court to no authority specifically holding that no right of action exists. There exists a dispute of fact whether Defendants satisfied their duty of honesty, good faith and fair dealing. (Continued in Part 2 of 2) =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC., A VS. MARTIN BARRENECHEA, AKA GUILLERMO MARTIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 29, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT GUILLERMO BARRENCHEA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion to set aside is granted. A trial court has broad discretion to vacate a default judgment. However, that discretion can be exercised only if defendant establishes a proper ground for relief, by the proper procedure, within time limits. (See Cruz v. Fagor America, Inc. (2006) 146 CA4th 488, 495.] Under Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 473, subd. (d), a court may set aside a default judgment which is valid on its face, but void, as a matter of law, due to improper service. (See Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 540.] A trial court has no statutory power to set aside a judgment that is not void once six months have elapsed since the entry of a judgment. (See Cruz v. Fagor America, Inc. (2006) 146 CA4th 488, 495-96.) A judgment is said to be void on its face when the invalidity is apparent upon an inspection of the judgment roll. Where defendant does not answer the complaint, the judgment roll includes the proof of service. (Id. at 496.) Here, 17 months have elapsed since the entry of default judgment on January 23, 2014. However, Defendant has provided evidence that his address at the time of service was in San Jose, California, not Sunnyvale, California as reflected in the proof of service. Further, Defendant was served by substituted service and by mail, he was not personally served. Therefore, this court finds service to be invalid. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: UBIKA SOLUTIONS, INC VS. ZINIO, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or Alternative Relief Based On Plaintiffs Refusal To Comply With Court Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday June 26, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT ZINIO, LLC'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or Alternative Relief Based On Plaintiffs Refusal To Comply With Court Order. (part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant and Cross Complainant ZINIO, LLC's unopposed Motion for Terminating Sanctions seeking dismissal of the Complaint and to strike the Answer to Cross Complaint filed by Plaintiff and Cross Defendant UBIKA SOLUTIONS, INC. (AKA VEREXO, INC.) is GRANTED. (See, Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 771,778; Cal. Civ. Pro. Section 2023.030(d)) The Court finds that Plaintiff and Cross Defendant UBIKA SOLUTIONS, INC. (AKA VEREXO, INC.) has ignored and failed to comply with two discovery Orders dated May 18, 2015 and June 9, 2015 compelling responses to Defendant and Cross Complainant ZINIO, LLC's Second Set of Form Interrogatories, and Second Set of Requests for Production served April 1, 2015. The Order in this matter will become effective on the 20th day following the service of Notice of Entry of Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein's Order arising from the June 9, 2015 hearing if no complete, verified responses without objection to Defendant and Cross Complainant ZINIO, LLC's Second Set of Form Interrogatories, and Second Set of Requests for Production served April 1, 2015 are received by Defendant and Cross Complainant ZINIO, LLC'. (continued on part 2) =(302/JPT)
